title: activity — entitlements
description: Five entitlement keys. Values seeded per Tenant by the entitlements module (from Subscription plan + BusinessType.default_config.entitlements). Enforcement lives in the observer / policy layers — a 402 response indicates the tenant needs to upgrade; distinct from 403 (permission-based refusal).

entitlements.description: Master gate. Available on every plan. When false (should never happen at steady state — this is the baseline), the LogsActivity trait no-ops on domain models. Included here so ops can freeze activity capture tenant-wide during an incident (e.g. a runaway write path).

refusedResponse.note: When off, activity capture silently no-ops — the trait's boot() detects and skips. No error surface.

entitlements.description: Extend retention past the 90-day default. When true, tenants may create ActivityRetentionPolicy rows with retention_days > 90 (up to 730 for regulator holds). Without this, every retention override is capped at 90 — the entitlement is the gate.

Real-time WebSocket streaming to the tenant.{id}.activity + user.{id}.activity channels. Without this, the feed still exists — the frontend just polls /api/v1/activities every 15s instead of subscribing to reverb. Small plan+.

refusedResponse.note: Not entitled = polling mode. The /activities/feed endpoint returns entitled=false + poll_interval_ms — the frontend adapts.

entitlements.description: Enables the opt-in daily / weekly digest email. Non-entitled tenants don't see the digest opt-in in /settings/notifications. Medium plan+.
Enables the /api/v1/activities/export endpoint + the 'Export CSV' button. Non-entitled tenants can still browse the feed via the paginated list endpoint. Medium plan+.
